productStatement: GrantLayer turns agentic grant workflows into verifiable institutional records.

readinessCategories.name: Connection Configuration
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Connection parameters are declared in configuration schema and validated on startup
readinessCategories.notes: Includes database URL, pool size, timeout, retry policy

readinessCategories.name: Credential Secret Source
readinessCategories.purpose: Database credentials sourced through approved secret boundary
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Credentials are never hard-coded; always resolved through secret source abstraction
readinessCategories.notes: Aligns with GL-068 secret management baseline design

readinessCategories.name: Schema Compatibility
readinessCategories.purpose: All Product Core tables, indexes, and constraints work on PostgreSQL
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Automated verification that every migration applies cleanly to PostgreSQL
readinessCategories.notes: SQLite-specific DDL must not leak into Product Core contract

readinessCategories.name: Migration Validation
readinessCategories.purpose: Every migration tested against both SQLite and PostgreSQL
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: CI runs migration validation against PostgreSQL for every migration file
readinessCategories.notes: Migrations must remain explicit, reviewable, and backward-compatible

readinessCategories.name: Transaction Semantics
readinessCategories.purpose: Grant workflows demonstrate correct transaction behavior on PostgreSQL
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Regression tests verify atomic grant creation, approval, and evidence persistence under failure
readinessCategories.notes: Includes rollback scenarios and mid-transaction failure handling

readinessCategories.name: CI PostgreSQL Service
readinessCategories.purpose: Test suite can execute against live PostgreSQL in CI or local environment
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: CI pipeline includes PostgreSQL-backed test execution with gating
readinessCategories.notes: Does not require production cloud database; local or containerized PostgreSQL acceptable

readinessCategories.name: Backup/Restore Expectations
readinessCategories.purpose: Documented retention, restore procedures, and recovery verification
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Runbooks exist and recovery tests confirm restoration within defined objectives
readinessCategories.notes: Aligned with GL-069 deployment package backup/restore configuration expectations

readinessCategories.name: Persistence Operational Runbook
readinessCategories.purpose: Operator guidance for database health, troubleshooting, and failover
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Runbook covers health checks, connection troubleshooting, and incident response
readinessCategories.notes: Must not contain credentials or secrets

readinessCategories.name: Production Fail-Closed Persistence Startup
readinessCategories.purpose: Production mode refuses to start if persistence config is missing or invalid
readinessCategories.futureValidationGate: Startup checks block production startup on missing backend declaration, invalid credentials, or unreachable database
readinessCategories.notes: Aligned with GL-066 runtime configuration and GL-069 deployment package expectations

failClosedRules: Production mode must refuse to start if database backend is not explicitly declared
Production mode must refuse to start if database credentials are missing or unresolved
Production mode must refuse to start if persistence backend is unreachable after retry
Production mode must refuse to start if schema version is unverified or incompatible
Production mode must not silently fall back to SQLite file or in-memory database
Production mode must not allow placeholder or default database credentials
migrationExpectations: Migrations must remain explicit and reviewable
Schema changes must not happen silently
Backward compatibility must be considered for product releases
Data migration risk must be separated from API behavior changes
transactionExpectations: Grant approval and grant creation flows require transactional clarity
Evidence persistence and verification records need consistent linkage
Audit/provenance records should not be partially inconsistent with workflow state
Future PostgreSQL work should include transaction semantics tests
backupRestoreRelationship: Persistence backend readiness is connected to backup/restore and data lifecycle design. Backup/restore is not implemented in this block. Production persistence must define retention, restore, and recovery expectations.
